seoTitle: How to Create a Form in Slack With Workflow Builder
metaDescription: Build an interactive intake form in Slack using Workflow Builder, add custom fields, and route responses to a dedicated channel.
intro: Collecting structured requests or feedback shouldn't mean sorting through messy chat threads. This guide shows how to build a form using Slack's Workflow Builder, add input fields, and route submissions into a designated channel.

steps.heading: Open Workflow Builder
steps.body: Click your workspace name at the top, then launch Workflow Builder from the tools menu.

steps.heading: Create a new workflow
steps.body: Click 'Create Workflow' and choose a link or shortcut trigger to control how the form gets accessed.

steps.heading: Add a form step
steps.body: Click 'Add Step' and select 'Collect info with a form' to start building your intake fields.

steps.heading: Configure your fields
steps.body: Add a clear form title and input fields like text boxes, dropdowns, or date selectors, marking required fields as needed.

steps.heading: Route responses and publish
steps.body: Add a follow-up step to send responses to a specific channel, then click Publish to activate the form.

faq.question: Can I require certain fields to be filled out?
faq.answer: Yes, toggle the required field checkbox for any input you don't want users to skip.

faq.question: Where do form responses go after submission?
faq.answer: You configure a routing step that sends responses to a channel, direct message, or connected tool of your choice.

faq.question: Can external users outside my workspace fill out the form?
faq.answer: Access depends on your trigger and sharing settings, so check your workflow's permissions if you need outside submissions.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many fields a form can have?
faq.answer: Workflow Builder supports multiple fields per form, though very long forms may be harder for users to complete quickly.
